Kamala Harris’ bid for the U.S. presidency in 2024 wasn’t a success, and her famous ex believes he knows why.
Montel Williams dated Harris more than two decades ago, when she was an attorney working for the city of San Francisco and he was one of the most popular daytime television hosts.
And although he always knew she’d go on to do big things, he was hardly surprised she lost the election to Donald Trump — a stance he defends on the latest episode of On Par With Maury Povich.
Vice President Harris entered the presidential race as the Democratic nominee only 107 days before the election, after 81-year-old incumbent Joe Biden bowed out in July 2024 due to concerns over his advanced age.
Harris, who published a memoir reflecting on her 2024 presidential run, 107 Days, has hinted at “possibly” tossing her hat into the 2028 election.

In a BBC interview, former vice-president says she isn’t done with politics and could ‘possibly’ be the next president. Kamala Harris said she was not done with politics and strongly suggested that she was considering another bid for president. In an interview scheduled to air on the BBC on Sunday morning, Harris said that she would “possibly” be the next president, making the clearest suggestion to date that she would make another run for the White House in 2028 despite lagging far behind in the polls. Harris insisted that she had not yet made a decision, but that she was confident that there would eventually be a woman in the White House and her political career was not over. “I am not done,” the former vice-president told the BBC. “If I listened to polls I would have not run for my first office, or my second office – and I certainly wouldn’t be sitting here,” she said.","score":0.87981987,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cx2n7k2veywo","title":"Kamala Harris tells BBC she may run for president again","content":"# 'I am not done' - Kamala Harris tells BBC she may run for president again. Former US Vice-President Kamala Harris has told the BBC she may run again for the White House. In her first UK interview, Harris said she would \"possibly\" be president one day and was confident there will be a woman in the White House in future. Making her strongest suggestion to date that she will make another presidential bid in 2028 after losing to Donald Trump last year, Harris dismissed polls that put her as an outsider to become the Democrats' pick for the next election. Speaking to Sunday with Laura Kuenssberg, Harris also turned her fire on her former rival, branding Trump a \"tyrant\", and said warnings she made about him on the campaign trail had been proved right. In the BBC interview Harris entertained the prospect of another run at the White House, saying her grandnieces would, \"in their lifetime, for sure\", see a female president.","score":0.8506799,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://abc7news.com/amp/post/former-vice-president-kamala-harris-leaves-door-open-2028-presidential-run-according-new-bbc-interview/18074008","title":"Former VP Kamala Harris leaves door open for 2028 presidential run ...","content":"In an interview with the BBC, Kamala Harris said she expects a woman will be president in the coming years, and it could \"possibly\" be her.","score":0.80442166,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.cnn.com/2025/10/25/politics/kamala-harris-presidential-run","title":"Kamala Harris hints at another presidential run: 'I am not done'","content":"Former Vice President Kamala Harris has hinted she could make another bid for the White House in an interview with the BBC, saying she would","score":0.6636041,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.facebook.com/CBSNews/posts/former-vice-president-kamala-harris-said-shes-thinking-about-running-for-the-202/1359620842696423","title":"Former vice president Kamala Harris said she's “thinking about” running ...","content":"I would love to see her win the election, but imo it will be too risky in 2028.
